factors.summary: Demand is decelerating due to lower energy prices and volume declines in several commodity groups.

evidence.text: Lower energy prices continue to negatively impact the demand for coal and shale related products. In addition to the declines in coal, frac sand, crude oil, and pipe shipments; volumes of grain, metals, and intermodal were down compared to 2015.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand is decelerating due to lower energy prices and volume declines in several commodity groups.

factors.summary: Pricing power is mixed with core pricing gains offset by declines in average revenue per car due to lower fuel surcharge revenue and negative business mix.

evidence.text: Freight revenues decreased 14%, or $749 million, in the first quarter compared to the same period in 2015. The decrease was due to an 8% decline in volume and a 6% decline in average revenue per car (ARC) resulting from lower fuel surcharge revenue and negative business mix. These declines were partially offset by core pricing gains.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Pricing power is mixed with core gains offset by declines in average revenue per car due to lower fuel surcharge revenue and negative business mix.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting with declines in several commodity groups offset by growth in automotive shipments.

evidence.text: Freight revenue from agricultural products shipments decreased in the first quarter of 2016 compared to 2015 as lower fuel surcharge revenue and volume declines were partially offset by core pricing gains. The strong U.S. dollar and high worldwide inventories reduced grain and grain products shipments by 5% and 6%, respectively, primarily due to lower demand for export feed grains, wheat, and other feed products.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting with declines in several commodity groups offset by growth in automotive shipments, indicating mixed signals.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating with a 14% year-over-year decrease in freight revenues.

evidence.text: Freight revenues decreased 14%, or $749 million, in the first quarter compared to the same period in 2015.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is decelerating with a 14% year-over-year decrease in freight revenues, indicating significant deterioration.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining across several commodity groups, driven by lower demand and market conditions.

evidence.text: Volume levels increased 6% compared to first quarter of 2015, driven by growth in Coal, Automotive, Agricultural Products, and Intermodal shipments.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are declining across several commodity groups, but evidence shows a 6% increase, suggesting mixed signals.

factors.summary: End markets are mixed with automotive shipments growing while coal and crude oil shipments are declining.

evidence.text: Strong automotive production and sales levels drove the volume growth, with stronger growth in automotive parts relative to growth in finished vehicles. Shipments out of the Powder River Basin (PRB) declined 38% in the first quarter of 2016 as a result of depressed coal markets due to low natural gas prices, mild winter weather in our served territory, and high inventory levels.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: End markets are mixed with automotive growth offset by declines in coal and crude oil shipments, indicating no clear trend.

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly fuel prices, are declining and impacting revenue.

evidence.text: Lower fuel prices continue to negatively impact the demand for coal and shale related products. Freight revenues from fuel surcharge programs were $113 million in the first quarter of 2016 compared to $447 million in the same period of 2015. Lower fuel surcharge revenue resulted from lower fuel prices, lower volumes, and a smaller fuel surcharge lag benefit compared to the first quarter of 2015.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Declining input costs, particularly fuel prices, are negatively impacting revenue, suggesting a headwind.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable with decreases in compensation and benefits driven by lower volume-related costs and productivity gains.

evidence.text: Compensation and benefits include wages, payroll taxes, health and welfare costs, pension costs, other postretirement benefits, and incentive costs. For the first quarter, expenses decreased 11%, driven by lower volume-related costs and productivity gains.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are decreasing due to lower volume-related costs and productivity gains, indicating a positive development.

factors.summary: Operating margins are compressing due to lower revenue and higher depreciation expense, despite cost reductions.

evidence.text: Our first quarter operating ratio increased 0.3 points to 65.1% in 2016 versus 2015 as core pricing gains, resource realignments, and productivity gains were more than offset by the impact of lower volume, inflation, and a larger fuel surcharge lag benefit realized in the first quarter of 2015.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Operating margins are compressing due to lower revenue and higher depreciation, despite cost reductions.

factors.summary: Cost reduction actions, including lower fuel prices and productivity gains, are delivering measurable savings.

evidence.text: Operating expenses decreased $495 million in the first quarter compared to 2015. Significantly lower fuel prices, volume-related savings, lower equipment maintenance expense, and productivity gains drove the decrease.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction actions, including lower fuel prices and productivity gains, are delivering measurable savings.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving with gains in average train speed and terminal dwell time.

evidence.text: Average train speed for the first quarter of 2016, as reported to the AAR, improved 11% compared to the same period in 2015. Average terminal dwell time in the first quarter of 2016 improved 7%, compared to the same period of 2015, reflecting the impact of lower volume and improved network operations.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity is improving with gains in average train speed and terminal dwell time.

factors.summary: The company is in contraction mode with a 11% decrease in average employees compared to the same period in 2015.

evidence.text: Employee levels in the first quarter decreased 11% compared to the same period in 2015 driven by lower volume levels, productivity gains, and fewer transportation employees in training.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company is in contraction mode with a 11% decrease in average employees compared to the same period in 2015.

factors.summary: Management appears cautious with actions to reduce costs and improve productivity despite lower demand.

evidence.text: Throughout the quarter, we continued our efforts to align our critical resources with current demand. At the end of the quarter, we had approximately 4,400 employees either furloughed or in alternate work status and approximately 1,400 locomotives in storage.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Management appears cautious with actions to reduce costs and improve productivity despite lower demand.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include continued declines in coal and shale-related products due to lower energy prices.

evidence.text: Lower energy prices continue to negatively impact the demand for coal and shale related products. In addition to the declines in coal, frac sand, crude oil, and pipe shipments; volumes of grain, metals, and intermodal were down compared to 2015.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include continued declines in coal and shale-related products due to lower energy prices.

factors.summary: Fuel prices and volume levels are key sensitivities impacting near-term results.

evidence.text: Lower fuel prices continue to negatively impact the demand for coal and shale related products. Freight revenues from fuel surcharge programs were $113 million in the first quarter of 2016 compared to $447 million in the same period of 2015. Lower fuel surcharge revenue resulted from lower fuel prices, lower volumes, and a smaller fuel surcharge lag benefit compared to the first quarter of 2015.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Fuel prices and volume levels are key sensitivities impacting near-term results.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is slightly increasing with adjusted debt to capital rising from 45.7% to 47.1%.

evidence.text: Adjusted debt to capital is a non-GAAP financial measure under SEC Regulation G and Item 10 of SEC Regulation S-K, and may not be defined and calculated by other companies in the same manner. We believe this measure is important to management and investors in evaluating the total amount of leverage in our capital structure, including off-balance sheet lease obligations, which we generally incur in connection with financing the acquisition of locomotives and freight cars and certain facilities.
evidence.subsection: Debt to Capital / Adjusted Debt to Capital

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Debt leverage is slightly increasing with adjusted debt to capital rising from 45.7% to 47.1%.
